# Questions in Livestream 128

Live Stream #128: Knowledge Gaps Filled Fast

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=333" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:33</a> | When did El become a generic word for God, and was it already generic when Yahweh was called El? | He says El was already a generic word for God long before that period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=436" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:16</a> | Why do many very early Exodus daters place the Exodus before 1500 BC or with a pharaoh whose real dates are before 1500 BC? | He says many alternative Exodus theories depend on chronological revisionism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=729" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:09</a> | If only scribes and priests were allowed in Egyptian temples, what about the pharaoh? | The king is the high priest of every Egyptian cult and was allowed in temple contexts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=852" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:12</a> | Who does he prefer, John Walton or Kenneth Kitchen? | He chooses Kitchen, while saying he also likes Walton and is biased because of his relationship with Kitchen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=980" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:20</a> | Was there a minimum age to be an heir designate? | No. The decisive factor was being the firstborn, usually the firstborn male. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=1088" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:08</a> | Could Egyptians have painted blood on their doorframes, or was Passover only available to Israelites? | He works from Exodus 12 and treats the warning and ritual as directed to Israel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=1357" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:37</a> | Did he see InspiringPhilosophy's debate with Eric Hovind, and what were his thoughts? | Yes. His reaction was sharply negative toward Hovind's performance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=2164" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:04</a> | What does he think of Seth Fleishman's suggestion that Amalek is the Lukka people? | He thinks the proposal has some plausibility. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=2229" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:09</a> | What does he make of ley lines and ancient holy buildings aligned to mystical energy lines? | He rejects ley lines as modern nonsense and does not think ancient builders used them that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=2373" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:33</a> | How do we know Saul of Tarsus was from Tarsus in Turkey rather than Tartessos in Spain? | He distinguishes Tarsus from Tartessos and says the evidence points to Tarsus in Cilicia. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=2614" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:34</a> | If Romans 5:12 says death is the result of sin, does infant death mean babies go to hell? | No. He argues that grace is extended and that children are not condemned for parental sin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=3284" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:44</a> | Does the archaeological record indicate that Gibeon was uninhabited during the time of Joshua? | He says Gibeon's record is complicated, but he thinks there was Late Bronze occupation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=3509" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:29</a> | Would he recommend Robert Alter's Art of Biblical Narrative, translation, and Hebrew Bible commentary? | He had not read the translation or Art of Biblical Narrative, so he keeps the recommendation limited. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=3679" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:19</a> | How did people in the Late Bronze Age view good and evil? | He says the categories were somewhat different and tied to ancient cultural and religious assumptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=4063" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:43</a> | Do Levantine religions have something parallel to the Ark of the Covenant, apart from Egyptian religion? | Not in the same way; he says there are parallels to cherubim, but Egyptian influence is more relevant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=4183" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:43</a> | If Israelite DNA is indistinguishable from Bronze Age Canaanite DNA, why should Israelites not have at least some Egyptian DNA? | He says DNA evidence does not require that conclusion and must be interpreted cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=4402" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:22</a> | How much chronological data does the Groundhog program have, and does it include New Testament anchor points? | It does not include the New Testament period; it was built around earlier chronological data. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=4884" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:24</a> | How does someone get involved in chronology? | He jokes that one has to be born into it, then stresses that chronology is extremely difficult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=5502" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:42</a> | What is the genre of Jonah, and was Jonah really eaten? | He says Jonah mixes genres and should not be reduced to only one simple category. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=5757" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:57</a> | Given shakeups in higher education, where are specialist fields like Egyptology and archaeology going? | He expects departments may become fewer and more consolidated, but the fields will not simply disappear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=5880" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:00</a> | Is archaeology a STEM field? | No. He says archaeology is still classed as a humanity or art, not a STEM field. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=6566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:26</a> | What is his opinion on Adam and Abraham's historicity? | He thinks both Adam and Abraham were historical figures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=6656" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:56</a> | Any thoughts on the new Oxyrhynchus sayings fragment and possible use of Luke, Matthew, or Thomas? | He had not seen the latest text and therefore answers only cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=6803" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:23</a> | Regarding creation ex nihilo, did the ancients think God made the waters too, or were they preexistent? | He treats ex nihilo as meaning God created even the waters, not merely brought order to preexistent stuff. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=7098" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:18</a> | Are Jesus and John the Baptist apocalyptic Jews, and does that imply they denied a soul separate from the body? | He says they believed in a soul and does not accept that implication. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=7402" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:22</a> | How did ancient Egyptian music work, did they notate it, and what instruments are shown? | He says we do not know exactly how the music worked, though instruments and scale theories can be discussed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=7628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:08</a> | What is his general view of how inspiration works, especially with Septuagint and Masoretic readings? | He applies his normal doctrine of inspiration while allowing textual criticism to compare manuscript traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=8437" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:37</a> | Does he plan to review the final excavation report from Khirbet el-Maqatir when the volume on biblical Ai appears? | He had not planned on it and gives reasons for caution about committing to that review. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=9159" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:39</a> | Why are flood stories so widespread if there is no scientific evidence for a global flood? | He says flood stories are widespread, but that does not by itself establish the specific global-flood claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=9621" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:21</a> | Has he read Mika Waltari's The Egyptian? | No. He says he has not read it and prefers working from the original texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=10519" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:19</a> | If Hyksos means foreign rulers, why would Hyksos kings use it as a title in cartouches and scarabs? | He says there was a tribal or group affiliation behind the term, so the title was not simply an insult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=10759" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:19</a> | What is his opinion of Michael Homan's To Your Tents, O Israel and views on the history of the Tabernacle? | He says there is a range of views, but he had not read Homan's book and keeps the answer qualified. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=11017" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:37</a> | Is it irredeemably heretical to believe the waters were always there? | He says yes, while allowing the questioner to explain a model elsewhere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=11425" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:25</a> | Does God ever lead someone to make art? | Yes. He says God can lead someone to make art and illustrates from his own experience. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=13338" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:42:18</a> | If Adam and Cain were farmers and Tubal-Cain worked bronze and iron, would that put early pre-flood times in the Bronze or Iron Age? | He says those labels cannot be mapped simply onto Genesis in that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=13536" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:36</a> | Could God have wanted people not to remember the sin of Noah's generation and left no flood evidence except the rainbow? | He says it is a difficult question and treats the premise cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=14616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:36</a> | Could the destruction of Hazor level 16 be contemporary with the Middle Bronze Jericho IV destruction? | No. He says those destructions are separated by about 200 years. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=14670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:04:30</a> | How can Deuteronomy's fortified-city language fit the thirteenth century BC rather than the Middle Bronze period? | He says the language is hyperbolic and that Late Bronze casemate walls fit the biblical descriptions better. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=14955" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:09:15</a> | Are the Dead Sea Scrolls authentic? | Most are, especially excavated and museum-held scrolls; he warns about forgeries on the antiquities market. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/SCiHmB9L850?t=15038" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:10:38</a> | Did archaeologists find Jericho's wall fallen outward? | He says that claim comes from interpretation of earlier excavations and that the issue is more complicated. |
